title: carex capilliformis
intro: Carex capilliformis, also known as the hair-like sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Carex genus. Native to North America, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Carex capilliformis include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions made from its roots and rhizomes, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
